title: Executive Oversight Dashboard — Mean Time to Acknowledge Critical Alerts

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF GV.OV-01 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF GV.OV-01 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.14: Executive Oversight Dashboard — Mean Time to Acknowledge Critical Alerts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.14' running on notable macro (urgency, status, owner, _time), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
search `notable` urgency=critical earliest=-90d
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_touch by rule_name, owner
| eval window_sec=last_touch-first_seen
description: Measures how quickly executives or duty managers acknowledge sev-1 security alerts, evidencing GV.OV oversight of outcomes.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Executive Oversight Dashboard — Mean Time to Acknowledge Critical Alerts. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Define critical urgency mapping; (2) use notable status transitions if available; (3) monthly governance review; (4) document remediation for outliers.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `notable` macro (urgency, status, owner, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define critical urgency mapping; (2) use notable status transitions if available; (3) monthly governance review; (4) document remediation for outliers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
search `notable` urgency=critical earliest=-90d
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_touch by rule_name, owner
| eval window_sec=last_touch-first_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Executive Oversight Dashboard — Mean Time to Acknowledge Critical Alerts (GV.OV-01)** — Measures how quickly executives or duty managers acknowledge sev-1 security alerts, evidencing GV.OV oversight of outcomes.

Documented **Data sources**: `notable` macro (urgency, status, owner, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rule_name, owner** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **window_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with ES Incident Review for the relevant correlation. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart, Table
knownFalsePositives: Identification gaps during new asset onboarding, M&A integration, or after refresh waves.
